summary: Decompiled a collection of small, straightforward functions across Kirby fighter code, ground/stage code, item code, menu code, and vi/universe screen files. Most changes replaced easy_func marker comments with thin wrappers around existing engine helpers, simple frees, one-line OnFrame callbacks, motion/collision glue, or prototype corrections. Review focused on header hygiene for the newly populated mndiagram3 module: include its own header from the C file and add a guard clause to the header.
what_changed: Implemented Kirby functions in ftKb_Init.c: fn_800F1FDC now gets the Fighter, calls ftCommon_8007D7FC, and changes motion state with Fighter_ChangeMotionState using current animation frame and frame speed; ftKb_MsSpecialNStart_Coll now checks ft_80082708(gobj) == GA_Ground and calls ftKb_SpecialNMs_8010B868.

Implemented ground/stage cleanup and callbacks: grBigBlueRoute_8020C210 frees gp->gv.corneria.xC8; grGreens_80213988 calls grAnime_801C8138 with the ground map_id; grGreens_80214804 frees gp->gv.corneria.xC8 and xCC.

Added ground generator glue: grHomeRun_8021C800 calls grZakoGenerator_801CAE04(0), and grZakoGenerator_801CADE0 returns grZakoGenerator_801CA8B4(1). grzakogenerator.h was updated from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures for grZakoGenerator_801CA8B4(int) and grZakoGenerator_801CADE0(void).

Implemented item physics/collision helpers for Yoshi egg variants and Leadead: itEvyoshiegg_UnkMotion3_Phys and itKyasarinegg_UnkMotion3_Phys apply fall speed and terminal fall speed from ItemAttr then call it_80274658 with it_804D6D28->x68_float; itKyasarinegg_UnkMotion3_Coll delegates to it_2725_Logic28_DmgDealt if it_8026DA08 succeeds; itLeadead_UnkMotion7_Phys updates velocity/floor-normal behavior using it_8027C8D0 and it_8027C0A8.

Implemented it_802B2B08 in ityoshieggthrow.c: reads the first f32 from article specialAttributes, changes item animation state with Item_80268E5C, then passes the attribute to it_80275158. Its header prototype was made concrete.
Populated mndiagram3.c and mndiagram3.h with fn_80246F0C, a simple HSD_Free wrapper, and added an include guard to the header after review.
Added simple vi OnFrame functions in vi0801.c, vi1101.c, vi1201v1.c, vi1201v2.c, and vi1202.c; each implemented callback calls vi_8031CAAC and required including vi.h.
Updated item headers to replace unknown signatures with concrete prototypes where implementations or callers clarified the parameter and return types.
smart_moves: Grouped many low-risk easy_funcs in one PR, allowing several comment markers to be replaced with short matching C implementations across subsystems.
Used existing inline accessors such as GET_FIGHTER, GET_GROUND, and GET_ITEM rather than open-coding object layout access.
Matched small destructor-like stage functions by identifying direct HSD_Free calls on ground gv.corneria fields.
Reused known item physics helper patterns: retrieve ItemAttr from GET_ITEM(gobj)->xCC_item_attr, call it_80272860 with fall speed values, then call it_80274658 with a shared float attribute.
For repeated vi screen callbacks, recognized the common OnFrame pattern as a trivial call to vi_8031CAAC and added vi.h includes rather than duplicating declarations.
Converted several UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations to precise signatures once the implementation made parameter and return types evident.
decomp_lessons: Easy_func sweeps are effective when functions are thin wrappers around already-named engine helpers, but each new C file should still follow normal header discipline.
When adding a function to a previously empty or near-empty module, include that module's own header in the .c file so the compiler checks the implementation against the exported prototype.
New headers should immediately get include guards, even for a single prototype.
Stage object cleanup functions often reveal themselves as HSD_Free calls on Ground gv fields after retrieving Ground with GET_GROUND.

Many item physics functions can be matched by copying known item patterns: pull ItemAttr from the item, use x10_fall_speed and x14_fall_speed_max, then apply shared per-item constants through existing physics helpers.
Simple OnFrame callbacks in vi/un files may be just calls into vi_8031CAAC; adding the correct vi.h include is preferable to relying on implicit or ad-hoc declarations.
Concrete prototypes in headers should be updated alongside decompiled bodies to reduce UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS debt and improve type checking for later work.
naming_conventions: Address-based unknown functions remain named with fn_<address> when no semantic name is established, e.g. fn_800F1FDC and fn_80246F0C.

Known subsystem prefixes were preserved: ftKb_ for Kirby fighter code, grBigBlueRoute_/grGreens_/grHomeRun_/grZakoGenerator_ for ground/stage code, itKyasarinegg_/itEvyoshiegg_/itLeadead_/itYoshieggthrow_ for item code, and un_<address>_OnFrame for vi/universe callbacks.
Header guards use the uppercase file/module name pattern, shown by MNDIAGRAM3_H.
Callback suffixes such as _Anim, _Phys, _Coll, _OnFrame, and _OnEnter were retained to describe lifecycle roles.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use accessor macros like GET_FIGHTER, GET_GROUND, and GET_ITEM to produce expected object dereferences and improve readability.

For motion-state wrappers, preserve exact constants and argument ordering to Fighter_ChangeMotionState; fn_800F1FDC uses motion id 0x15F and flags 0x0C4C5082U with fp->cur_anim_frame and fp->frame_speed_mul.

For boolean/collision wrappers, match early conditional structure directly, as in itKyasarinegg_UnkMotion3_Coll returning it_2725_Logic28_DmgDealt(gobj) only when it_8026DA08(gobj) is nonzero and returning 0 otherwise.

For trivial wrapper functions, direct calls with literal arguments can be enough to match, e.g. grHomeRun_8021C800 -> grZakoGenerator_801CAE04(0) and grZakoGenerator_801CADE0 -> grZakoGenerator_801CA8B4(1).
Header prototype corrections are part of matching work: replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with concrete return and parameter types can be necessary once a body is decompiled.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.resolution: The final diff includes #include "mndiagram3.h" before baselib/memory.h.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.resolution: The final diff adds #ifndef MNDIAGRAM3_H / #define MNDIAGRAM3_H / #endif around the fn_80246F0C prototype.

review_feedback.author: Gelatart
review_feedback.resolution: Author acknowledged the requested mndiagram3.c include change.

review_feedback.author: Gelatart
review_feedback.feedback: Like this? #ifndef MNDIAGRAM3_H #define MNDIAGRAM3_H void fn_80246F0C(void* arg0); #endif
review_feedback.resolution: The submitted header guard matches the reviewer request.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename fn_800F1FDC or fn_80246F0C to semantic names.
Search for additional vi OnFrame callbacks that are simple wrappers around vi_8031CAAC.
Search for more item physics functions using it_80272860 with ItemAttr x10_fall_speed and x14_fall_speed_max.
Look for later cleanup of grZakoGenerator_801CA8B4 return type, since this PR uses long in the header while grZakoGenerator_801CADE0 returns s32.
Find other new or previously empty headers that need include guards and self-inclusion from their corresponding .c files.
